instruction: Design a sleep improvement plan for someone struggling with insomnia. The plan must include pre-sleep routines, diet adjustments, and recommended apps. Ensure the response contains exactly 300 words, with each paragraph containing at least 50 words. Use the phrase "sleep hygiene" exactly three times and "circadian rhythm" at least twice. The keyword "relaxation" must appear before "dietary." Present the pre-sleep routines in a bulleted list with exactly five points, each containing at least two sentences and starting with the phrase "To improve sleep." Write the response in the second person, maintaining a supportive and encouraging tone throughout. The response must contain at least seven paragraphs.
llm_response:
You are taking a brave and important step toward reclaiming your restful nights by creating a structured plan tailored specifically for your unique needs. This journey requires patience, consistency, and a gentle approach to changing your habits over time so that your body can finally learn to rest deeply. Remember that every small change you make today builds a stronger foundation for better health tomorrow, and you are not alone in this process.

To improve sleep hygiene, you must first understand how your internal clock works and how to align it with the natural world around you. Your circadian rhythm acts as a biological guide that tells your body when to wake up and when to feel tired, so respecting this cycle is essential. By adjusting your daily schedule to match sunlight patterns, you signal to your brain that it is time to wind down and prepare for a good night's rest.

To improve sleep hygiene, you should establish a calming pre-sleep routine that signals to your mind that the day is officially over and rest is beginning. To improve sleep hygiene, you need to create a consistent environment that promotes deep relaxation and minimizes any external distractions that might keep you awake. To improve sleep hygiene, you must avoid bright screens at least an hour before bed to prevent blue light from disrupting your natural sleep patterns. To improve sleep hygiene, you should engage in quiet activities like reading a physical book or listening to soft music to lower your heart rate. To improve sleep hygiene, you must keep your bedroom cool, dark, and quiet to ensure your body can enter the deep stages of restorative sleep easily.

Before you focus on dietary changes, prioritize a dedicated relaxation practice such as deep breathing or progressive muscle release to calm your nervous system. Your dietary adjustments should include avoiding heavy meals, caffeine, and alcohol close to bedtime, as these substances can interfere with your ability to fall asleep quickly. Instead, try consuming warm milk or herbal tea containing chamomile, which are known to soothe the digestive system and promote a sense of calm throughout your evening.

Tracking your progress with helpful mobile applications can provide valuable insights into your sleep patterns and help you identify triggers that may be keeping you awake at night. Apps like Sleep Cycle or Calm offer guided meditations and smart alarms that wake you up gently during your lightest sleep phase, ensuring you feel refreshed upon waking. These tools also allow you to monitor your sleep quality over time, giving you data to adjust your routine for even better results in the future.

Consistency is key when trying to reset your circadian rhythm, so try to go to bed and wake up at the same time every single day, even on weekends. This regularity helps train your brain to expect sleep at a specific time, making it easier to fall asleep faster and stay asleep longer without frequent awakenings. Be kind to yourself if you have a bad night, as occasional disruptions are normal and do not mean your efforts have failed or that you are doing something wrong.

You have the power to transform your sleep habits and wake up feeling energized and ready to tackle each new day with enthusiasm and clarity. Trust the process and celebrate every small victory along the way, knowing that your dedication to better sleep will yield significant improvements in your overall well-being. Keep moving forward with confidence, and soon you will find that peaceful nights become the new normal for your life.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The model failed to follow several constraints, including the word count constraint (it is significantly longer than 300 words), the paragraph count constraint (it has 7 paragraphs, but the content structure is off), and the specific formatting constraint for the bulleted list (it used a block of text instead of a bulleted list). Most importantly, the model did not acknowledge any conflicting constraints (though there were no obvious logical conflicts in the instruction, it failed to meet the strict quantitative constraints). Since the model simply attempted to fulfill the instruction without addressing the difficulty of meeting the exact word count and paragraph constraints simultaneously, it falls under category 1.

**Answer**: 1
